title: STARTELEMENT
html:
STARTELEMENT

Add to My Topics
Add selected topic and subtopicsAdd Topic & Subtopics with topic headAdd entire publication

Download PDF
Save selected topicSave selected topic and subtopicsSave entire publication
Share
Email LinkCopy Link
Feedback
  • Last UpdatedJuli 17, 2024
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

Operation activated by the SAX parser when a start tag is encountered.

Use the following syntax if namespace has already been enabled (with STARTPREFIXELEMENT or ENDPREFIXELEMENT), or if namespace support is not required:

operation STARTELEMENT
params
     string Name: in
     string Attributes: in
endparams

Implement your code here

end; STARTELEMENT

Use the following syntax if you want to enable namespace support:

operation STARTELEMENT
params
  string Name: in
  string QName: in
  string URI: in
  string Attributes: in
endparams

Implement your code here

end; STARTELEMENT

Parameters

Table 1. Compatiblity Mode Parameters
Parameter Description
Name Name of the tag.
Attributes Associated list of attributes or an empty string. All normal ProcScript instructions that operate on associated lists can be used to process this list, such as getitem /id.
Table 2. Namespace Parameters
Parameter Description
Name Unqualified tag name, which is the tag name without the prefix.
QName Fully-qualified tag name (that is, the tag name including the prefix).
URI Namespace with which prefix was previously associated, either in the same tag or in an earlier tag for which the end tag has not yet been parsed.
Attributes Associated list of attributes or an empty string. All normal ProcScript instructions that operate on associated lists can be used to process this list, such as getitem/id.

Description

This operation is called by PARSE and PARSEFILE for each start tag encountered. It can be implemented with two parameters for both non-namespace and namespace enabled parsing, or with four parameters to enable namespace parsing.

If the ENDPREFIXMAPPING operation is defined, STARTELEMENT is called after ENDPREFIXMAPPING.

Compatibility Mode

Attributes are provided in the Attributes parameter as a Uniface list: "name1=value1;name2=value2;...".

For example, when <part> is encountered, Attributes is empty. When <parts nr="12" lang="english"> is encountered, it contains the list: "nr=12;lang=english"

However, for the following example, parsing depends on whether namespace support has been enabled (by a previous callback to STARTPREFIXMAPPING, ENDPREFIXMAPPING, STARTELEMENT or ENDELEMENT):

<a:y xmlns="http://www.mywebsite.com/default"
     xmlns:a="http://www.mywebsite.com/a.html"
     xmlns:b="http://www.mywebsite.com/b.html">>

If namespace support is not enabled, the three xmlns specifications are just normal attributes, and the Attributes will contain this Uniface list:

"xmlns=http://www.mywebsite.com/default; xmlns:a=http://www.mywebsite.com/a.html; xmlns:b=http://www.mywebsite.com/b.html"

If namespace support is enabled, then these specifications will already have been processed by the STARTPREFIXMAPPING operation and the Attributes parameter will be empty.

Namespace Mode

To understand how STARTELEMENT works when it is implemented in namespace mode, consider the following XML start tag.

<a:y a:y xmlns="http://www.mywebsite.com/default" 
 
 >
 <aaa atr1="AAA" atr1="BBB">BBB</aaa>
</a:y>

For element a:y:

  • Name is y
  • QName is a:y
  • URI is "http://www.mywebsite.com/a.html", which is the namespace associated with the a prefix
  • Attributes is "" because the xmlns specifications are not considered attributes with namespace support enabled.

For element aaa, which has no namespace prefixes:

  • Name is aaa
  • QName is aaa
  • URI is "http://www.mywebsite.com/default", because there is a default namespace defined in the previous element a:y. (If this were not the case, URI would be "".)
  • Attributes is a Uniface list with sublists, in which each sublist describes an attribute. For each attribute, the following sublist items are defined:
Table 3. Attribute Sublist Items
Parameter Description
QNAME Fully-qualified attribute, including the prefix, if any.
NAME Unqualified attribute name, without the prefix.
URI Namespace associated with the prefix to which this attribute belongs.
TYPE Type of the attribute; one of CDATA, NMTOKEN, NMTOKENS, Enumeration, ENTITY, ENTITIES, ID, IDREF , IDREFS, or NOTATION.

For descriptions of these values, see https://docstore.mik.ua/orelly/xml/xmlnut/ch03_03.htm
VALUE Value of the attribute (after the = sign) in the XML document.

In the following example, Attributes is:

QNAME=a:atr1!;NAME=atr1!;URI=http://www.mywebsite.com/a.html!;TYPE=CDATA!;VALUE=AAA;b:QNAME=atr1!;NAME=atr1!;URI=http://www.mywebsite.com/b.html!;TYPE=CDATA!;VALUE=BBB

To get the value specified in the XML document for b:atr1, extract the sublist for the second attribute, b:atr1, from Attributes, and then get the specific value:

operation STARTELEMENT
params
 string Name: in
 string QName: in
 string URI: in
 string Attributes: in
endparams
variables
 AttList = "" 
 vAttItem = ""
endvariables
getitem vAttList, Attributes, 2
getitem/id vAttItem, vAttList, "VALUE"
end; STARTELEMENT

In the same way, you can get the URI, qualified and unqualified name, and its type from each attribute’s sublist.

Related Links
  • Compatibility Mode

    Review how attributes work in Compatibility Mode.

  • Namespace Mode

    To understand how STARTELEMENT works when it is implemented in namespace mode, consider the following XML start tag.

  • PARSE

    Parses a specified XML stream.

  • PARSEFILE

    Parses an XML document in the specified file.

  • ENDELEMENT

    Operation activated by the SAX parser when it encounters an end tag.

  • STARTPREFIXMAPPING

    Operation activated by the SAX parser when it encounters a start tag with one or more xmlns (namespace) specifications.

  • ENDPREFIXMAPPING

    Operation activated by the SAX parser for each namespace specification in a start tag, when the parser encounters the corresponding end tag.

Use the following syntax if namespace has already been enabled (with STARTPREFIXELEMENT or ENDPREFIXELEMENT ), or if namespace support is not required: Explain this code operation STARTELEMENT params string Name: in string Attributes: in endparams Implement your code here end; STARTELEMENT Copy Use the following syntax if you want to enable namespace support: Explain this code operation STARTELEMENT params string Name: in string QName: in string URI: in string Attributes: in endparams Implement your code here end; STARTELEMENT Copy Parameters Table 1. Compatiblity Mode Parameters Parameter Description Name Name of the tag. Attributes Associated list of attributes or an empty string. All normal ProcScript instructions that operate on associated lists can be used to process this list, such as getitem /id . Table 2. Namespace Parameters Parameter Description Name Unqualified tag name, which is the tag name without the prefix. QName Fully-qualified tag name (that is, the tag name including the prefix). URI Namespace with which prefix was previously associated, either in the same tag or in an earlier tag for which the end tag has not yet been parsed. Attributes Associated list of attributes or an empty string. All normal ProcScript instructions that operate on associated lists can be used to process this list, such as getitem/id . Description This operation is called by PARSE and PARSEFILE for each start tag encountered. It can be implemented with two parameters for both non-namespace and namespace enabled parsing, or with four parameters to enable namespace parsing. If the ENDPREFIXMAPPING operation is defined, STARTELEMENT is called after ENDPREFIXMAPPING . Compatibility Mode Attributes are provided in the Attributes parameter as a Uniface list: "name1=value1 ; name2=value2 ; ..." . For example, when <part> is encountered, Attributes is empty. When <parts nr="12" lang="english"> is encountered, it contains the list: "nr=12 ; lang=english" However, for the following example, parsing depends on whether namespace support has been enabled (by a previous callback to STARTPREFIXMAPPING , ENDPREFIXMAPPING , STARTELEMENT or ENDELEMENT ): Explain this code <a:y xmlns="http://www.mywebsite.com/default" xmlns:a="http://www.mywebsite.com/a.html" xmlns:b="http://www.mywebsite.com/b.html">> Copy If namespace support is not enabled, the three xmlns specifications are just normal attributes, and the Attributes will contain this Uniface list: Explain this code "xmlns=http://www.mywebsite.com/default ; xmlns:a=http://www.mywebsite.com/a.html ; xmlns:b=http://www.mywebsite.com/b.html" Copy If namespace support is enabled, then these specifications will already have been processed by the STARTPREFIXMAPPING operation and the Attributes parameter will be empty. Namespace Mode To understand how STARTELEMENT works when it is implemented in namespace mode, consider the following XML start tag. Explain this code <a:y a:y xmlns="http://www.mywebsite.com/default" > <aaa atr1="AAA" atr1="BBB">BBB</aaa> </a:y> Copy For element a:y : Name is y QName is a:y URI is "http://www.mywebsite.com/a.html" , which is the namespace associated with the a prefix Attributes is "" because the xmlns specifications are not considered attributes with namespace support enabled. For element aaa , which has no namespace prefixes: Name is aaa QName is aaa URI is "http://www.mywebsite.com/default" , because there is a default namespace defined in the previous element a:y . (If this were not the case, URI would be "" .) Attributes is a Uniface list with sublists, in which each sublist describes an attribute. For each attribute, the following sublist items are defined: Table 3. Attribute Sublist Items Parameter Description QNAME Fully-qualified attribute, including the prefix, if any. NAME Unqualified attribute name, without the prefix. URI Namespace associated with the prefix to which this attribute belongs. TYPE Type of the attribute; one of CDATA , NMTOKEN , NMTOKENS , Enumeration , ENTITY , ENTITIES , ID , IDREF , IDREFS , or NOTATION . For descriptions of these values, see https://docstore.mik.ua/orelly/xml/xmlnut/ch03_03.htm VALUE Value of the attribute (after the = sign) in the XML document. In the following example, Attributes is: Explain this code QNAME=a:atr1!;NAME=atr1!;URI=http://www.mywebsite.com/a.html!;TYPE=CDATA!;VALUE=AAA;b:QNAME=atr1!;NAME=atr1!;URI=http://www.mywebsite.com/b.html!;TYPE=CDATA!;VALUE=BBB Copy To get the value specified in the XML document for b:atr1 , extract the sublist for the second attribute, b:atr1 , from Attributes , and then get the specific value: Explain this code operation STARTELEMENT params string Name: in string QName: in string URI: in string Attributes: in endparams variables AttList = "" vAttItem = "" endvariables getitem vAttList, Attributes, 2 getitem/id vAttItem, vAttList, "VALUE" end; STARTELEMENT Copy In the same way, you can get the URI, qualified and unqualified name, and its type from each attribute’s sublist. Namespace Mode To understand how STARTELEMENT works when it is implemented in namespace mode, consider the following XML start tag. PARSE Parses a specified XML stream.
